mnemonics.title: ALU Operations Selection
mnemonics.content: SELECT LINES COUNT: TO FIND NUMBER OF SELECT LINES, CALCULATE 2^N >= NUMBER OF OPERATIONS, SOLVE FOR N. REMEMBER: 2 BITS = 4 OPERATIONS, 3 BITS = 8 OPERATIONS, 4 BITS = 16 OPERATIONS.

mnemonics.title: Full Adder Truth Table
mnemonics.content: CARRY AND SUM: SUM IS XOR OF ALL THREE INPUTS (A XOR B XOR CIN). CARRY IS MAJORITY FUNCTION: COUT = (A AND B) OR (B AND CIN) OR (A AND CIN).

mnemonics.title: Status Flags Memory
mnemonics.content: FLAGS ACRONYM: CZVS - CARRY, ZERO, OVERFLOW, SIGN. REMEMBER: SIGN = MSB OF RESULT, ZERO = ALL BITS ZERO, CARRY = UNSIGNED OVERFLOW, OVERFLOW = SIGNED OVERFLOW.

mnemonics.title: Two's Complement Subtraction
mnemonics.content: SUBTRACTION METHOD: FLIP ALL BITS (ONES COMPLEMENT) THEN ADD 1 - THIS IS TWOS COMPLEMENT. ADD THIS TO MINUEND FOR RESULT.

mnemonics.title: Carry Look-Ahead Concepts
mnemonics.content: GENERATE (GI) = AI AND BI (CARRY ALWAYS PRODUCED). PROPAGATE (PI) = AI XOR BI (CARRY IN BECOMES CARRY OUT). CARRY OUT = GI OR (PI AND CARRY IN).

acronyms.usage: The core processor component that performs arithmetic and logical operations on binary data.
Circuit that selects between multiple inputs based on select lines, used in ALU to choose operation result.
Combinational circuit adding three inputs (A, B, Carry-in) producing Sum and Carry-out.
Basic adder circuit adding two inputs (A, B) producing Sum and Carry.
Fast adder design that computes carries in parallel using generate and propagate signals.
Serial connection of full adders where carry propagates through each stage.
Register containing CPU status flags including Zero, Carry, Overflow, and Sign flags.
Input carry to a stage and output carry from a stage in addition operations.

visualTips.visualization: IMAGINE DOMINOES FALLING IN A LINE - EACH DOMINO (FULL ADDER) FALLS ONLY AFTER THE PREVIOUS ONE FALLS, CAUSING A RIPPLE EFFECT THROUGH THE CHAIN. THE CARRY PROPAGATES LIKE WAVES.
THINK OF A RAILWAY SWITCH - THE SELECT LINES DETERMINE WHICH TRACK (INPUT) CONNECT TO THE MAIN LINE (OUTPUT). ONLY ONE TRACK IS ACTIVE AT A TIME.
IMAGINE TWO STAGES: FIRST A HALF ADDER ADDS A AND B, THEN ANOTHER HALF ADDER ADDS THE RESULT TO CIN. THE OR GATE COMBINES THE TWO CARRIES. THIS IS CALLED CASCADE STRUCTURE.
IMAGINE A TEAM OF WORKERS WHERE EACH WORKER CAN CALCULATE THEIR CARRY WITHOUT WAITING FOR OTHERS - THEY ALL WORK IN PARALLEL. THIS ELIMINATES THE WAITING CHAIN.
TO NEGATE A NUMBER: START FROM RIGHTMOST BIT, COPY ALL ZEROS, THEN FLIP THE FIRST 1 YOU SEE, THEN INVERT ALL BITS TO THE LEFT. THIS IS THE FASTEST METHOD.

analogies.analogy: THE ALU IS LIKE A SCIENTIFIC CALCULATOR THAT CAN PERFORM ADDITION, SUBTRACTION, MULTIPLICATION, DIVISION, AND LOGICAL TESTS (GREATER THAN, EQUAL TO). THE SELECT LINES ARE LIKE THE BUTTON YOU PRESS TO CHOOSE WHICH OPERATION.

STATUS FLAGS ARE LIKE WARNING LIGHTS ON A CAR DASHBOARD. ZERO FLAG IS LIKE A 'TANK EMPTY' LIGHT (RESULT = 0). OVERFLOW IS LIKE AN 'ENGINE OVERHEATING' LIGHT (PROBLEM DETECTED). CARRY IS LIKE A 'BRAKE WEAR' INDICATOR.
A FULL ADDER IS LIKE A SINGLE DIGIT IN MANUAL ADDITION. TO ADD MULTI-DIGIT NUMBERS, YOU CHAIN THESE TOGETHER, EACH PASSING THE CARRY TO THE NEXT DIGIT - EXACTLY LIKE CARRYING IN PRIMARY SCHOOL MATH.
REGISTERS ARE LIKE THE HANDS THAT HOLD NUMBERS TO BE CALCULATED. THE ALU IS LIKE THE BRAIN THAT PERFORMS THE CALCULATION. THE CONTROL UNIT IS LIKE THE TEACHER TELLING EVERYONE WHAT TO DO AND WHEN.

SELECT LINES IN ALU ARE LIKE A ROTARY DIAL ON A MULTI-FUNCTION KITCHEN APPLIANCE. DEPENDING ON WHERE YOU SET THE DIAL, THE SAME MOTOR (ALU) WILL BLEND, CHOP, OR WHIP - DIFFERENT OUTPUT FROM SAME HARDWARE.
